content:
4 min read
5 hours ago

--

Hot Module Replacement (HMR) is a development feature that allows an application to replace only the modified module or component at runtime without performing a full page reload.
Instead of restarting the entire application, the development server sends the updated module to the browser, which patches the running application.
Hot Module Replacement (HMR) is a development capability that updates only the modified component or module in a running application instead of reloading the entire page. Before HMR, developers relied on live reload, where every code change caused a full application restart, losing runtime state such as form inputs or navigation context. HMR addresses this by recompiling only the changed module, notifying the browser through the development server, and replacing the affected module at runtime. This significantly shortens the feedback loop and improves developer productivity, especially in large Angular applications with hundreds of components. It’s important to note that HMR is a development-only feature; production builds do not include it because its purpose is to improve the developer experience rather than application runtime performance.
Run
ng serve or npm startnpm start
> demo@0.0.0 start
> ng serveComponent HMR has been enabled, see https://angular.dev/hmr for more info.
The message:
Component HMR has been enabled, see https://angular.dev/hmr for more info.is not coming from your application code. It is printed by the Angular CLI (ng serve) itself.
Where does it come from?
When you run:
npm startyour package.json probably contains:
{
"scripts": {
"start": "ng serve"
}
}ng serve starts the Angular development server, and in recent Angular versions (Angular 17+), Component HMR (Hot Module Replacement) is enabled by default.
During startup, the Angular CLI prints:
Component HMR has been enabled, see https://angular.dev/hmr for more info.This log originates from the Angular Dev Server (@angular/build / previously @angular-devkit/build-angular), not from your project's source files.
Why was HMR introduced?
Before HMR, development relied on Live Reload.
Traditional Development Flow (Live Reload)
Developer saves file
│
▼
Angular recompiles
│
▼
Browser refreshes
│
▼
Application bootstraps again
│
▼
All runtime state is lostProblems:

• Entire application reloads

• User login state may reset

• Form data is lost

• Navigation resets

• Long startup time for large applications

• Slower developer feedback loop
Development Flow with HMR
Developer saves file
│
▼
Angular recompiles changed component
│
▼
Webpack/Vite sends updated module
│
▼
Browser replaces only that module
│
▼
Application continues runningResult:

• No full page refresh

• Faster updates

• Better developer productivity

• State preservation when possible
Why not simply refresh the browser?
A full refresh has significant overhead.
Imagine an enterprise banking application.
Application StartupLoad JS bundles
↓
Initialize Angular
↓
Create DI container
↓
Load Routes
↓
Initialize Services
↓
HTTP Calls
↓
Authentication
↓
Render UI
Every save repeats this work.
HMR skips almost all of it.
Only the changed component is replaced.
Internal Architecture
Developer
│
Save File
│
▼
Angular Compiler
│
Rebuild Changed Module
│
▼
HMR Runtime
│
WebSocket
│
▼
Browser
│
Replace Module
│
Continue ExecutionNotice that the browser doesn’t perform a hard reload.
How does Angular HMR work?
Modern Angular uses the underlying development tooling (historically webpack, and now newer builders) together with Angular’s runtime support.
High-level process:

• Detect file change

• Compile only affected files

• Create updated JavaScript module

• Notify browser via WebSocket

• Browser downloads new module

• Angular replaces the component

• UI updates
Why is HMR faster?
Suppose your application has
600 Components
120 Services
40 Lazy ModulesYou modify
HeaderComponent.htmlLive Reload
Restart entire application600 Components
120 Services
40 Modules
HMR
Only
HeaderComponentHuge performance improvement.
When does HMR preserve state?
Example:
Profile Component
Name : Piyali
Email: xyz@gmail.comDeveloper changes CSS.
Without HMR
Page reload
Name:
Email:Data disappears.
With HMR
Name : Piyali
Email: xyz@gmail.comState is preserved when Angular can safely update the component.
Limitations
HMR is not magic.
A full reload may still happen when:

• Global configuration changes

• angular.json changes

• Environment configuration changes

• Build configuration changes

• Some dependency updates

• Changes requiring a fresh application bootstrap
Is HMR used in Production?
No.
HMR is strictly a development feature.
Production builds are optimized for:

• Tree shaking

• Ahead-of-Time (AOT) compilation

• Minification

• Code splitting

• Caching

• Performance
The HMR runtime is not included in production builds.
Does Angular automatically refresh?
Yes. Angular has long supported live reload, which automatically refreshes the application after changes.
Modern Angular (v17+ and continuing in later versions) also enables Component HMR by default during development. That’s why you see:
Component HMR has been enabledSo, there are two mechanisms:
| Feature | Live Reload | HMR |
| ----------------------------- | ----------- | ----------------- |
| Detects file changes | ✅ | ✅ |
| Updates browser automatically | ✅ | ✅ |
| Full page refresh | ✅ | ❌ (usually) |
| Preserves component state | ❌ | ✅ (when possible) |
| Faster development | Good | Better |HMR is not just automatic refresh. Angular already had automatic refresh (live reload). HMR is an enhancement that updates only the changed components without reloading the entire page whenever possible, making development faster and helping preserve application state.
Is it configured somewhere?
Check your angular.json.
You may have something like:
"serve": {
"builder": "@angular/build:dev-server"
}or
"serve": {
"builder": "@angular-devkit/build-angular:dev-server"
}There is usually no explicit "hmr": true because component HMR is enabled by default in development.
Can I disable it?
Yes.
Run:
ng serve --no-hmror modify your package.json:
{
"scripts": {
"start": "ng serve --no-hmr"
}
}
